summary: Matched sysdolphin baselib particle-system function psInitAppSRT in C, removing its assembly stub from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.s. The PR also cleaned up psAppSRT-related declarations by replacing an erroneous unk_t typedef for HSD_PSAppSRT with a real struct typedef, moving/typing _psAppSRT more concretely, adding public prototypes in psappsrt.h, and introducing the _generator struct plus auxiliary emitter-shape structs used by later psAppSRT code.

key_files.role: Added the matched C implementation of psInitAppSRT and replaced local extern stubs/unk_t-based declarations with concrete globals needed by the function.
Removed the psInitAppSRT assembly implementation after matching it in C.
Added prototypes for psInitAppSRT, psAddGeneratorAppSRT, and HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C.

Fixed psAppSRT typing, added HSD_psAppSRT/HSD_Particle/HSD_Generator typedefs, moved the _psAppSRT definition earlier, and added _generator plus auxDisc/auxLine/auxTornado/auxRect/auxCone/auxSphere structs.

what_changed: Implemented psInitAppSRT(int num, int size) in C. The matched function zeros hsd_804D78DE and hsd_804D78D8, stores size into HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958[0] as u16, initializes HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 with HSD_ObjAllocInit(&HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0, size, 4U), and returns false/0.
Removed the assembly version of psInitAppSRT from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.s, including the decomp.me scratch note that reported a previous 74.69% match attempt.
Deleted several local extern declarations from psappsrt.c that used unk_t or duplicated psAddGeneratorAppSRT, narrowing the C file to the globals required for psInitAppSRT.
Added psInitAppSRT, psAddGeneratorAppSRT, and HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C declarations to psappsrt.h.
Removed the erroneous typedef 'typedef unk_t HSD_PSAppSRT;' and replaced it with a real forward declaration/typedef pattern for struct _psAppSRT as HSD_psAppSRT.
Added forward declarations and typedefs for struct _particle as HSD_Particle and struct _generator as HSD_Generator.
Changed _psAppSRT's next pointer and freefunc argument to use struct _psAppSRT* instead of the previous unk_t-derived HSD_PSAppSRT alias.

Added the _generator struct, including fields for generator linkage, kind/random/count, HSD_JObj pointer, life/type/bank/link/texture/id fields, cmdList, transform/velocity/physics values, child count, appsrt pointer, HSD_PSUserFunc, callback, and an aux union.
Added auxiliary generator parameter structs auxDisc, auxLine, auxTornado, auxRect, auxCone, and auxSphere.
smart_moves: The match was achieved by correcting type information rather than forcing code shape with opaque unk_t declarations.
Replacing the bogus HSD_PSAppSRT unk_t alias with a real struct typedef made _psAppSRT self-referential fields and callbacks more accurate and reusable.
Adding _generator in psstructs.h prepared shared type information for later psAppSRT functions, reducing future reliance on local guessed structs.
Moving prototypes into psappsrt.h reduced duplicate/local extern declarations in psappsrt.c and made function signatures available to other translation units.
The simple C body mirrors the assembly operations directly: two SDA u16 clears, one u16 array store, one HSD_ObjAllocInit call with alignment/count argument 4U, then return 0.
decomp_lessons: Small allocator-init functions often match cleanly when global variables are typed exactly enough; here u16 globals/arrays and HSD_ObjAllocData were sufficient.
Avoid typedefing known-but-incomplete structs to unk_t. A forward-declared struct pointer is usually more accurate and can unblock self-referential structs and callback signatures.
When matching code in a subsystem, invest in shared headers before adding more C functions; later particle/generator functions can reuse the same HSD_Generator, HSD_Particle, and HSD_psAppSRT types.

Function parameters may not all be used by the matched code. psInitAppSRT takes two ints in the new declaration, but the visible implementation only uses size; this matches the assembly where r4 is stored and r3 is not otherwise used.
Use concrete return types when the assembly returns literal zero through r3; this PR models psInitAppSRT as bool returning 0/false.
naming_conventions: Particle-system app SRT struct was named struct _psAppSRT with typedef HSD_psAppSRT, notably using lowercase 'ps' in the typedef as shown in the diff.
Particle and generator structs follow baselib style: struct _particle typedefed to HSD_Particle and struct _generator typedefed to HSD_Generator.
Auxiliary generator parameter structs use lowercase typedef names matching the shape: auxDisc, auxLine, auxTornado, auxRect, auxCone, auxSphere.
Existing unknown/global symbol names were preserved for unresolved data and functions, such as hsd_804D78DE, hsd_804D78D8, HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958, HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0, and HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C.
Struct fields use short particle-system source-style names such as genLife, linkNo, texGroup, cmdList, numChild, appsrt, userfunc, and aux.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the psInitAppSRT assembly stub after the C version matched.
Matched the assembly's store-halfword instructions by declaring hsd_804D78DE and hsd_804D78D8 as extern u16 and casting size to u16 for HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958[0].
Matched the HSD_ObjAllocInit call by passing the object allocator data address, the size parameter, and literal 4U.
Preserved the assembly behavior of returning zero by returning 0 from a bool function.
Evidence from the removed asm indicates an earlier decomp.me scratch for psInitAppSRT was at 405 / 74.69%, and this PR completed the match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use the newly added struct _generator fields to match psAddGeneratorAppSRT or related psAppSRT functions.
Search for uses of HSD_psAppSRT versus HSD_PSAppSRT to confirm typedef naming consistency across baselib particle-system headers.
Check whether auxDisc/auxLine/auxTornado/auxRect/auxCone/auxSphere field names were later refined from debug symbols or additional matches.
Review subsequent cleanup for hsd_804D78DE, hsd_804D78D8, HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958, and HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 to see if these globals gained semantic names.
